```text
1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"flag"
7		"log"
8		"os"
9		"os/signal"
10		"syscall"
11		"time"
12	
13		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
14		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
15	
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/server"
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/version"
20	)
21	
22	func main() {
23		addr := flag.String("addr", ":2222", "SSH listen address")
24		dbPath := flag.String("db", "data/bbs.db", "SQLite database path")
25		hostkey := flag.String("hostkey", ".ssh/host_ed25519", "SSH host key path")
26		flag.Parse()
27	
28		st, err := store.Open(*dbPath)
29		if err != nil {
30			log.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
31		}
32		defer st.Close()
33		if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(context.Background()); err != nil {
34			log.Fatalf("seed boards: %v", err)
35		}
36		log.Printf("storage ready at %s", *dbPath)
37	
38		broker := chat.NewBroker()
39	
40		srv, err := server.New(server.Config{Addr: *addr, HostKey: *hostkey}, st, broker)
41		if err != nil {
42			log.Fatalf("server.New: %v", err)
43		}
44	
45		ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
46		defer stop()
47	
48		errCh := make(chan error, 1)
49		go func() {
50			log.Printf("sshbbs %s listening on %s", version.Version, *addr)
51			errCh <- srv.ListenAndServe()
52		}()
53	
54		select {
55		case err := <-errCh:
56			if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, ssh.ErrServerClosed) {
57				log.Fatalf("listen: %v", err)
58			}
59		case <-ctx.Done():
60			log.Println("shutdown signal received")
61			shutdown(srv, broker)
62		}
63	}
64	
65	// shutdown closes the listener (refuses new connections), then asks every
66	// live bubbletea program to quit, then closes the SSH server hard. The DB
67	// closes on the deferred st.Close() in main.
68	func shutdown(srv *ssh.Server, broker *chat.Broker) {
69		const drainTimeout = 3 * time.Second
70	
71		// 1. Stop accepting new connections.
72		listenerCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), drainTimeout)
73		defer cancel()
74		if err := srv.Shutdown(listenerCtx); err != nil {
75			log.Printf("server.Shutdown: %v", err)
76		}
77	
78		// 2. Tell every active session to quit.
79		sessions := broker.SessionsSnapshot()
80		if len(sessions) == 0 {
81			return
82		}
83		log.Printf("draining %d active session(s)", len(sessions))
84		for _, s := range sessions {
85			s.Program.Send(tea.Quit())
86		}
87	
88		// 3. Give them a moment to flush their final view, then force-close.
89		timer := time.NewTimer(drainTimeout)
90		defer timer.Stop()
91		for {
92			if len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()) == 0 {
93				log.Println("all sessions drained")
94				return
95			}
96			select {
97			case <-timer.C:
98				log.Printf("drain timeout; %d session(s) still attached", len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()))
99				return
100			case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5	
6		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/log"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/activeterm"
11		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/bubbletea"
12		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/logging"
13		"github.com/muesli/termenv"
14	
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/tui"
18	)
19	
20	type Config struct {
21		Addr    string
22		HostKey string
23	}
24	
25	func New(cfg Config, st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) (*ssh.Server, error) {
26		return wish.NewServer(
27			wish.WithAddress(cfg.Addr),
28			wish.WithHostKeyPath(cfg.HostKey),
29			wish.WithPasswordAuth(PasswordAuth(st)),
30			wish.WithMiddleware(
31				bubbletea.MiddlewareWithProgramHandler(makeProgramHandler(st, broker), termenv.ANSI256),
32				activeterm.Middleware(),
33				logging.Middleware(),
34			),
35		)
36	}
37	
38	// makeProgramHandler is the wish ProgramHandler. It owns *tea.Program
39	// creation so we can hand the pointer to the chat broker for live message
40	// delivery, and arrange Unregister when the SSH session ends.
41	func makeProgramHandler(st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) bubbletea.ProgramHandler {
42		return func(sess ssh.Session) *tea.Program {
43			ctx := sess.Context()
44	
45			// Register-mode: SSH user was "new". No DB user, no broker registration.
46			if reg, ok := ctx.Value(ctxKeyRegister).(bool); ok && reg {
47				deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, Broker: broker, IsRegister: true}
48				m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
49				return tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
50			}
51	
52			uid, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyUserID).(int64)
53			if uid == 0 {
54				log.Warn("session has no user_id; rejecting", "remote", sess.RemoteAddr())
55				return nil
56			}
57			user, err := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), uid)
58			if err != nil {
59				log.Error("load user", "id", uid, "err", err)
60				return nil
61			}
62	
63			deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: broker}
64			m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
65			p := tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
66	
67			// Hook session into the broker so other users can send to us.
68			cs := &chat.Session{UserID: user.ID, UserIDStr: user.UserID, Program: p}
69			broker.Register(cs)
70			log.Info("session registered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
71	
72			// Drain unread water balloons from DB and replay them as toasts.
73			// We do this *before* the program starts so the messages land in
74			// the model's first Update(); tea.Program.Send buffers if needed.
75			go func() {
76				unread, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(context.Background(), user.ID)
77				if err != nil {
78					log.Warn("drain unread WBs", "user", user.UserID, "err", err)
79					return
80				}
81				for _, w := range unread {
82					p.Send(tui.WBIncomingMsg{ID: w.ID, FromUserID: w.FromUserIDStr, Body: w.Body})
83					_ = st.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), w.ID)
84				}
85			}()
86	
87			// On session end, unregister so we stop trying to deliver to a dead Program.
88			go func() {
89				<-ctx.Done()
90				broker.Unregister(user.ID, p)
91				log.Info("session unregistered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
92			}()
93	
94			return p
95		}
96	}
97
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"time"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8	
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
14	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
15	type Deps struct {
16		Store      *store.Store
17		Broker     *chat.Broker // nil during register
18		User       *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister
19		IsRegister bool
20	}
21	
22	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
23	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
24	// and the toast footer.
25	type Root struct {
26		deps   Deps
27		sub    tea.Model
28		width  int
29		height int
30	
31		toast      string
32		toastUntil time.Time
33	}
34	
35	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
36	
37	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
38	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
39		r := Root{deps: deps}
40		if deps.IsRegister {
41			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
42		} else {
43			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
44		}
45		return r
46	}
47	
48	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
49		return m.sub.Init()
50	}
51	
52	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
53		switch msg := msg.(type) {
54		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
55			m.width = msg.Width
56			m.height = msg.Height
57			var cmd tea.Cmd
58			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
59			return m, cmd
60	
61		case tea.KeyMsg:
62			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
63			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
64				return m, tea.Quit
65			}
66			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
67			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
68				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
69			}
70	
71		case NavigateMsg:
72			return m.navigate(msg)
73	
74		case ErrorMsg:
75			if msg.Err != nil {
76				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
77			}
78			return m, nil
79	
80		case WBIncomingMsg:
81			m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
82			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect.
83			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
84				go func(id int64) {
85					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
86				}(msg.ID)
87			}
88			// fall through so the active screen can also react
89		}
90	
91		var cmd tea.Cmd
92		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
93		return m, cmd
94	}
95	
96	func (m Root) View() string {
97		v := m.sub.View()
98		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
99			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
100		}
101		return v
102	}
103	
104	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
105		m.toast = s
106		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
107	}
108	
109	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
110		var sub tea.Model
111		switch n.To {
112		case ScreenMainMenu:
113			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
114		case ScreenBoardList:
115			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
116		case ScreenBoardView:
117			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
118		case ScreenArticleView:
119			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
120		case ScreenPostCompose:
121			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
122		case ScreenWBInbox:
123			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
124		case ScreenWBCompose:
125			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
126		default:
127			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
128			return m, nil
129		}
130		m.sub = sub
131		cmd := sub.Init()
132		if m.width > 0 {
133			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
134			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
135			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
136			if cmd2 != nil {
137				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
138			}
139		}
140		return m, cmd
141	}
142

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"fmt"
7		"sync"
8	
9		_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
10	)
11	
12	// Store wraps the *sql.DB plus a process-level write mutex used for
13	// multi-statement writes that need to be atomic at the application level
14	// (e.g. inserting a push and recomputing the cached recommend_score).
15	// SQLite WAL handles concurrent reads; this mutex avoids SQLITE_BUSY under
16	// modest contention without spreading sql.Tx everywhere.
17	type Store struct {
18		db      *sql.DB
19		writeMu sync.Mutex
20	
21		users          *UserRepo
22		boards         *BoardRepo
23		articles       *ArticleRepo
24		pushes         *PushRepo
25		waterBalloons  *WaterBalloonRepo
26	}
27	
28	func Open(path string) (*Store, error) {
29		dsn := fmt.Sprintf(
30			"file:%s?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=foreign_keys(on)&_pragma=busy_timeout(5000)",
31			path,
32		)
33		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
34		if err != nil {
35			return nil, fmt.Errorf("open sqlite: %w", err)
36		}
37		if err := db.PingContext(context.Background()); err != nil {
38			_ = db.Close()
39			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ping sqlite: %w", err)
40		}
41		if err := apply(context.Background(), db); err != nil {
42			_ = db.Close()
43			return nil, fmt.Errorf("migrate: %w", err)
44		}
45	
46		s := &Store{db: db}
47		s.users = &UserRepo{s: s}
48		s.boards = &BoardRepo{s: s}
49		s.articles = &ArticleRepo{s: s}
50		s.pushes = &PushRepo{s: s}
51		s.waterBalloons = &WaterBalloonRepo{s: s}
52		return s, nil
53	}
54	
55	func (s *Store) Close() error { return s.db.Close() }
56	
57	// DB exposes the raw handle for callers that need it (tests, ad-hoc SQL).
58	// Production code should go through repos.
59	func (s *Store) DB() *sql.DB { return s.db }
60	
61	func (s *Store) Users() *UserRepo                 { return s.users }
62	func (s *Store) Boards() *BoardRepo               { return s.boards }
63	func (s *Store) Articles() *ArticleRepo           { return s.articles }
64	func (s *Store) Pushes() *PushRepo                { return s.pushes }
65	func (s *Store) WaterBalloons() *WaterBalloonRepo { return s.waterBalloons }
66

Perfect. I now have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings into a concise report.

---

## Territory Map: Mailbox Feature

**1. Migration Pattern**

- **Location**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/`
- **Naming**: `NNNN_name.sql` format (e.g., `0001_init.sql`, `0002_seed_boards.sql`). Next migration should be `0003_add_mailbox.sql`.
- **Discovery**: `migrate.go` (line 13) embeds migrations via `//go:embed migrations/*.sql`; `apply()` (line 20) reads the directory, parses version numbers from filenames before underscores (line 102–112), tracks applied versions in `schema_migrations` table (line 21–28), and runs pending migrations in ascending version order within individual transactions (line 58–66).
- **Content style**: Raw SQL with pragma setup (line 7 of 0001), DDL, indexes. Seed migrations use INSERT statements.

**2. WaterBalloon Repo + Schema**

- **Schema** (0001_init.sql, lines 57–67):
  ```sql
  CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
      id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
      from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,
      to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
      body            TEXT NOT NULL,
      delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      read_at         DATETIME,
      created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
  );
  CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
  ```
- **Repo file**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/waterballoons.go`
- **Key methods**:
  - `Insert(ctx, fromID, fromUserID, toID, body, deliveredLive)` → `*WaterBalloon` (line 34–54): acquires writeMu lock, returns full row with LastInsertId.
  - `ListUnreadFor(ctx, toUserID)` → `[]*WaterBalloon` (line 58–76): unread only, oldest-first by ID (ASC) for replay ordering.
  - `ListInboxFor(ctx, toUserID, limit)` → `[]*WaterBalloon` (line 80–102): unread first (DESC), then read newest-first, ordered by `(read_at IS NULL) DESC, id DESC`.
  - `MarkRead(ctx, id)` → error (line 104–111): writeMu lock, sets `read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` where still unread.
  - `MarkAllReadFor(ctx, toUserID)` → error (line 113–121): bulk mark read.
- **Scanning pattern** (line 24–32): `scanWB()` helper converts row into struct, converts int64 to bool for `DeliveredLive`.

**3. WaterBalloon Screen Pattern**

- **File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_wb.go`
- **Two models**:
  - `wbInboxModel` (line 20–124): displays list with date, from-user, status (NEW/·), body; cursor navigation (↑/↓/j/k); Enter/r/l/→ to reply; c to compose.
  - `wbComposeModel` (line 135–291): two-field form (to user, body); Tab to switch focus; Ctrl+S to send; success screen on sent.
- **Constants** (line 130–133): `wbFocusTo`, `wbFocusBody` focus indices.
- **Initialization** (line 29–37, 146–168): `newWBInboxModel()` calls `ListInboxFor()` and marks all read; `newWBComposeModel()` accepts pre-filled recipient.
- **Navigation**: KeyMsg handlers emit `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox}` or `{To: ScreenWBCompose, Recipient: ...}` (lines 49, 59, 66, 181, 185).

**4. Storage Test Helper**

- **File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/storetest/storetest.go`
- **`New(tb)`** (line 17–26): creates temp dir via `tb.TempDir()`, calls `store.Open()`, runs migrations, registers Cleanup for Close, returns `*store.Store`.
- **Example test**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/waterballoons_test.go`
  - Setup pattern (e.g., line 10–21): `st := storetest.New(t)`, then `storetest.MustUser()` for identities.
  - Exercise: calls repo methods (Insert, ListUnreadFor, MarkRead) directly.
  - Verify: slice assertions and ordering checks (lines 27–35, 78–108).
- **Helper functions** (storetest.go, lines 30–49): `MustUser()` creates user; `MustBoard()` fetches seeded board.

**5. Repo Wiring into App**

- **Main entry** (`cmd/sshbbs/main.go`, line 28): `st, err := store.Open(dbPath)` opens Store and applies migrations.
- **Server creation** (line 40): `server.New(config, st, broker)` — Store is passed to `server.New()`.

# Plan: P1 batch — PTT-feel polish, Online list, Article broadcast, Mailbox

## Context

P0 (MUST HAVE) shipped — see `## Done` in `TODO.md`. The next batch is the
five items in `## P1` of `TODO.md`: three `[S]` polish items plus two `[M]`
features. They share the same goal — make the BBS *feel* like PTT once
you're past register/login: keys do what muscle memory expects, you can see
who's online, and you have a real persistent inbox.

The exploration confirmed these can ship in one batch without rework:

- All keybinding work plugs into the existing per-screen tables; tests
  follow the established `[]string{"esc","backspace","left","h"}` table
  pattern in `internal/tui/screen_*_test.go`.
- The "send to everyone, filter on receive" broadcast pattern in
  `screen_article_view.go` (PushAddedMsg) is a direct template for the
  new `ArticleAddedMsg`.
- `WaterBalloonRepo` + `screen_wb.go` is a near-perfect blueprint for
  `MailRepo` + `screen_mail_*.go` — the only structural addition is
  threading (`parent_id` / `thread_id`).
- `chat.Broker.OnlineList()` already returns `[]OnlineUser` and is
  already tested — the online-user screen is purely a TUI shell.

Phases are written so each is independently shippable. You can stop
after Phase 1 if scope creeps; Phase 2 items don't depend on each other.

---

## Phase 1 — Quick wins (three `[S]` items)

### 1.1 PTT-style extra hotkeys

**Goal**: `[`/`]` (prev/next), `r` (reply on board view), `Q` (quit-to-menu)
on every list/menu screen, mirroring `j`/`k`/`h`/`l` placement.

**Files to modify** (key handler + table-driven tests):

- `internal/tui/screen_board_list.go` — add `[`/`]` aliases for `k`/`j`,
  `Q` returns `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu}`. (`r` not applicable here.)
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:40-73` — add `[`/`]`, `Q`, and
  `r` (open post compose targeting current article? or new top-level
  reply — see open question below).
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go` — add `[`/`]` (prev/next article
  in same board — needs `BoardRepo`/`ArticleRepo` neighbour lookup;
  if neighbour-fetch is too involved, scope `[`/`]` to list screens only
  this batch and leave article-view sibling nav for next).
- `internal/tui/screen_main_menu.go` — `Q` already covered by item 3;
  no change needed here unless we want `Q` from anywhere → main menu.

**Tests** (extend existing tables, no new files):

- `internal/tui/screen_board_list_test.go:34-48` — extend the back-key
  and forward-key tables with the new aliases.
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view_test.go` — same pattern.
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view_test.go` — same pattern.

**Open question**: what does `r` mean on board view vs article view?
On PTT, `r` from the article list opens the article (read), and `R` is
reply. On article view, `X` is push and `r`/`R` are different. Confirm
the intent before binding (see Open Questions section).

### 1.2 Article pagination — `g` / `G`

`PgUp`/`PgDn` already exist as `b`/`pgup` and space/`pgdown` aliases
(see `screen_article_view.go:79-88`). Only `g` (top) and `G` (bottom)
are missing.

**Files to modify**:

- `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:79-88` — add `case "g":
  m.scroll = 0` and `case "G": m.scroll = max(0, len(bodyLines) -
  viewportHeight)`. Compute `viewportHeight` the same way the existing
  paging keys do.

**Tests**:

- `internal/tui/screen_article_view_test.go` — add a table for scroll
  keys (`["g","G","b","pgup","space","pgdown"]`) asserting the resulting
  `m.scroll` value against a fixture article with N body lines.

### 1.3 Online-user list screen

**Files to create**:

- `internal/tui/screen_online.go` — new model `onlineModel`. Init pulls
  `m.deps.Broker.OnlineList()` (returns `[]chat.OnlineUser`), renders
  one line per user: `userid (N sessions)`. Cursor `j`/`k`, `h`/esc back
  to main menu, `l`/enter optionally opens a water-balloon compose
  pre-filled with the recipient (mirrors `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose,
  Recipient: ...}`).

**Files to modify**:

- `internal/tui/messages.go:3-23` — add `ScreenOnline` constant.
- `internal/tui/root.go:109-141` — add a `case ScreenOnline:` arm in
  `navigate()` that constructs `newOnlineModel(m.deps)`.
- `internal/tui/screen_main_menu.go:22-30` — insert as **slot 3**
  ("線上使用者 Online"); bump Quit to **slot 4**. Update the numeric
  shortcut handler accordingly.
- `internal/tui/screen_main_menu_test.go:110-132` — extend the numeric
  shortcut table to cover the new slot 3 → ScreenOnline mapping and
  slot 4 → Quit.

**Tests**:

- `internal/tui/screen_online_test.go` — new file. Use a fake broker
  (or stub `OnlineList()` via a small interface in `Deps` if needed —
  check whether the current `Deps.Broker` is concrete or interface;
  if concrete, pass through anyway and pre-register fake sessions
  with a real `chat.Broker` in tests, mirroring `broker_test.go`'s
  `fakeSender` approach).
- Cover: empty list rendering, cursor movement, enter-to-WB-compose
  navigation, h/esc back to main menu.

---

## Phase 2 — Two `[M]` features

### 2.1 Live broadcast of new articles (`ArticleAddedMsg`)

Direct mirror of the `PushAddedMsg` pattern.

**Files to modify**:

- `internal/tui/messages.go:36-44` — add:
  \```go
  type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
      BoardID    int64
      ArticleID  int64
      AuthorUserID string
      Title      string
  }
  \```
- `internal/tui/screen_post_compose.go:101-124` (`submit`) —
  - Capture the `*Article` returned by `Articles().Create()` (currently
    discarded on line 114).
  - After `IncrementPosts` succeeds, call
    `m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleAddedMsg{BoardID: m.boardID,
    ArticleID: art.ID, AuthorUserID: u.UserID, Title: art.Title})`
    if `m.deps.Broker != nil`.
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go` — handle `ArticleAddedMsg`:
  \```go
  case ArticleAddedMsg:
      if msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
          // re-fetch article list from DB (canonical state)
          if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID); err == nil {
              m.articles = arts
          }
      }
  \```
  Mirrors the `PushAddedMsg` re-fetch pattern in `screen_article_view.go:54-66`.

**Tests**:

- `internal/tui/screen_post_compose_test.go` — assert that a successful
  submit returns a `tea.BatchMsg` (or sequence) that includes both the
  `NavigateMsg` and a broker `SendToAll` invocation. Use a fake broker
  satisfying `chat.Sender`-like semantics (or expose a recording broker
  via the existing `Sender` interface).
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view_test.go` — feed an `ArticleAddedMsg`
  with matching `BoardID` and assert `m.articles` was re-fetched (one
  more entry); feed a non-matching `BoardID` and assert no change.

### 2.2 Mailbox 站內信

Persistent threaded messages — distinct from water balloons (transient
toast). Direct structural analog of the WaterBalloon repo + screens, plus
a thread/parent column.

**Files to create**:

- `internal/store/migrations/0003_add_mail.sql`:
  \```sql
  CREATE TABLE mail (
      id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      thread_id       INTEGER,                  -- root mail id; NULL ⇒ self-root
      parent_id       INTEGER REFERENCES mail(id),
      from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
      from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,
      to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
      subject         TEXT NOT NULL,
      body            TEXT NOT NULL,
      read_at         DATETIME,
      created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
  );
  CREATE INDEX idx_mail_to_unread ON mail(to_user_id, read_at);
  CREATE INDEX idx_mail_thread ON mail(thread_id);
  \```
  On insert, if `parent_id IS NULL` then `thread_id = id` (set with a
  follow-up UPDATE inside the same transaction, like `PushRepo.Create`'s
  two-statement pattern).

- `internal/store/mail.go` — `MailRepo` mirroring `WaterBalloonRepo`
  (see `internal/store/waterballoons.go`):
  - `Insert(ctx, fromID, fromUserID, toID, subject, body, parentID *int64) (*Mail, error)`
  - `ListInboxFor(ctx, toUserID, limit int) ([]*Mail, error)` — unread first,
    ordered by `(read_at IS NULL) DESC, id DESC`
  - `ListThread(ctx, threadID int64) ([]*Mail, error)` — chronological
  - `MarkRead(ctx, id int64) error`
  - `MarkAllReadFor(ctx, toUserID int64) error`
  - All write methods grab `Store.writeMu` (process-level mutex
    discipline — see `CLAUDE.md` for why).

- `internal/tui/screen_mail_inbox.go` — list view (subject, from,
  date, status). Cursor `j`/`k`, enter/`l` opens thread view, `c`
  composes new, `r` replies to highlighted, `h`/esc back.
- `internal/tui/screen_mail_thread.go` — chronological list of one
  thread; `r` to reply (pre-fills `parent_id` and `subject` with `Re:`
  prefix if not already present); `h`/esc back to inbox.
- `internal/tui/screen_mail_compose.go` — three-field form: To,
  Subject, Body (vs WB's two-field). `Tab` cycles focus, `Ctrl+S`
  sends. Optionally pre-fill recipient/parent_id from a NavigateMsg.

**Files to modify**:

- `internal/store/store.go:25,51,65` — add `mail *MailRepo` field,
  initialize in `Open()`, add `Mail()` accessor.
- `internal/tui/messages.go` — add `ScreenMailInbox`, `ScreenMailThread`,
  `ScreenMailCompose` constants. Extend `NavigateMsg` with `MailID` and
  `MailThreadID` fields if the existing ones (`BoardID`, `ArticleID`,
  `Recipient`) don't suffice for thread-view routing.
- `internal/tui/root.go:109-141` — add `case ScreenMailInbox/Thread/Compose`
  arms.
- `internal/tui/screen_main_menu.go` — add a "信箱 Mail" item (slot 3
  if 1.3 hasn't shipped; slot 4 otherwise; bump Quit accordingly).
- `internal/server/server.go` — on session login, fetch unread mail
  count via `MailRepo.CountUnreadFor` and surface as a one-time toast
  on the main menu (mirrors the on-reconnect water balloon replay
  pattern at server.go).

**Tests**:

- `internal/store/mail_test.go` — mirrors `waterballoons_test.go`:
  Insert (root + reply), thread-id self-fix on root, ListInboxFor
  ordering, ListThread chronological, MarkRead, MarkAllReadFor.
  Use `storetest.New(t)` and `storetest.MustUser()`.
- `internal/tui/screen_mail_inbox_test.go`,
  `internal/tui/screen_mail_thread_test.go`,
  `internal/tui/screen_mail_compose_test.go` — `Update()` tests
  mirroring `screen_wb_test.go`. Cover keybinding tables, navigation,
  send-success flow.

---

## Open questions to resolve before coding

1. **`r` semantics on board view vs article view** (Phase 1.1) — PTT's
   convention: on the article list `r` is "read article" (same as Enter)
   and `R` is "reply"; on article view, `X`/`x` is push and `r`/`R`
   replies. Do we want to follow that? If so, `r` on `screen_board_view`
   should NOT be a separate "reply" key — Enter/`l` already opens the
   article. This may mean we drop "r on board view" from the TODO.
2. **Article-view sibling nav** (Phase 1.1) — do `[`/`]` on
   `screen_article_view.go` mean "prev/next article in this board"
   (requires fetching neighbouring article IDs)? If yes, this is one
   small new repo method (`ArticleRepo.NeighboursOf(boardID, articleID)`).
   If we want to ship Phase 1 fast, scope `[`/`]` to list screens only.
3. **Mailbox unread badge on main menu** — do we want a `(N)` suffix on
   the "信箱 Mail" label when unread count > 0? Adds a periodic refresh
   or a broker `MailAddedMsg` listener on the main menu.

- **Phase 2.1**: alice on `screen_board_view` of board X; bob posts

  list does NOT refresh.
- **Phase 2.2**: alice sends a mail to bob; bob disconnects, reconnects,
  sees unread on main menu, reads in inbox; bob replies; alice sees the
  thread on next inbox open. Concurrent insert × 50 race test on
  `MailRepo.Insert` mirrors `TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`.

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**P1 has 5 items (3 [S] + 2 [M]). Which scope should the implementation cover?**

Options:
- **All P1 (3×[S] + 2×[M]) (Recommended)** - Phase 1 (hotkeys + g/G + online list) and Phase 2 (article broadcast + mailbox). One coherent batch; mailbox needs a new migration but doesn't depend on friends/blocklist.
- **Phase 1 only ([S] items)** - Just the 3 quick wins: PTT hotkeys, article g/G, online-user list. No schema changes, smallest review surface, ship today.
- **Phase 1 + 2.1 (skip mailbox)** - Add live broadcast of new articles. Defers mailbox (the only [M] item with a new table + 3 new screens) to a later batch.
- **Phase 2 only ([M] items)** - Skip the polish items, focus on the two larger features: ArticleAddedMsg broadcast and the mailbox.

**On 'board view', what should `r` do? PTT convention is `r` = read (same as Enter) on lists, and `R` = reply on the article view itself — so binding `r` as a separate 'reply' shortcut on board view may conflict.**

Options:
- **Drop `r` from board view (Recommended)** - Follow PTT convention: Enter/l already opens the article; binding r to also-open is redundant and binding it to 'reply' clashes with the conventional R-on-article-view meaning. Keep `[`/`]` and `Q` on board view; defer `r`/`R` reply to article view in a later batch.
- **`r` = same as Enter (read article)** - Match PTT: r becomes another alias for opening the highlighted article. Cheap, harmless, but somewhat redundant with Enter/l.
- **`r` = jump to post-compose for a reply article** - Treat r as a fast-path to the post-compose screen pre-filled with `Re: ` in the title. Diverges from PTT's R-on-article-view semantics but is what the original TODO line literally said.

**On article view, should `[`/`]` move to the prev/next article in the same board?**

Options:
- **Yes — add ArticleRepo.NeighboursOf (Recommended)** - Adds one small repo method (`NeighboursOf(boardID, articleID) (prev, next int64)`) and a NavigateMsg-based jump. Matches PTT muscle memory and is the main reason these keys are useful.
- **No — list screens only** - Bind `[`/`]` only on board list / board view (cursor up/down aliases). Article-view sibling navigation gets deferred to a later batch. Smaller diff, ships faster.


**Answer:** All P1 (3×[S] + 2×[M]) (Recommended), Drop `r` from board view (Recommended), Yes — add ArticleRepo.NeighboursOf (Recommended)
</details></tool-use>

# Plan: P1 batch — PTT-feel polish, Online list, Article broadcast, Mailbox

## Context

P0 (MUST HAVE) shipped — see `## Done` in `TODO.md`. The next batch is the
five items in `## P1` of `TODO.md`: three `[S]` polish items plus two `[M]`
features. They share the same goal — make the BBS *feel* like PTT once
you're past register/login: keys do what muscle memory expects, you can see
who's online, and you have a real persistent inbox.

The exploration confirmed these can ship in one batch without rework:

- All keybinding work plugs into the existing per-screen tables; tests
  follow the established `[]string{"esc","backspace","left","h"}` table
  pattern in `internal/tui/screen_*_test.go`.
- The "send to everyone, filter on receive" broadcast pattern in
  `screen_article_view.go` (PushAddedMsg) is a direct template for the
  new `ArticleAddedMsg`.
- `WaterBalloonRepo` + `screen_wb.go` is a near-perfect blueprint for
  `MailRepo` + `screen_mail_*.go` — the only structural addition is
  threading (`parent_id` / `thread_id`).
- `chat.Broker.OnlineList()` already returns `[]OnlineUser` and is
  already tested — the online-user screen is purely a TUI shell.

Phases are written so each is independently shippable. You can stop
after Phase 1 if scope creeps; Phase 2 items don't depend on each other.

---

## Phase 1 — Quick wins (three `[S]` items)

### 1.1 PTT-style extra hotkeys

**Goal**: `[`/`]` (prev/next) and `Q` (quit-to-menu) on every list/menu
screen. `r` is **dropped** from board view (PTT convention reserves `r`
for "read" on lists and `R` for reply on the article view; binding our
own meaning would clash with muscle memory).

**Files to modify** (key handler + table-driven tests):

- `internal/tui/screen_board_list.go` — add `[`/`]` aliases for
  cursor up/down; `Q` returns `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu}`.
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go:40-73` — add `[`/`]` aliases for
  cursor up/down; `Q` → main menu.
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go` — add `[`/`]` for **prev/next
  sibling article** in the same board (see new repo method below); `Q`
  → main menu.
- `internal/store/articles.go` — add
  `NeighboursOf(ctx, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error)`.
  Two `SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id=? AND id <? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`
  / `> ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1` queries; returns 0 sentinel when at the
  edge. Mind any soft-delete column once P2 ships (currently no filter
  needed).
- `internal/tui/messages.go` — `[`/`]` on article view emits
  `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, BoardID: ..., ArticleID: prev/next}`;
  current `NavigateMsg` already has `BoardID` and `ArticleID` — no
  schema change.

**Tests** (extend existing tables, no new files except for the repo):

- `internal/tui/screen_board_list_test.go:34-48` — extend the back-key
  and forward-key tables with the new aliases (`[` and `]`); add a
  separate test for `Q` → ScreenMainMenu.
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view_test.go` — same pattern.
- `internal/tui/screen_article_view_test.go` — table covering `[`/`]`
  navigation across a fixture of three articles in one board: at the
  middle article both keys produce a NavigateMsg with the right
  ArticleID; at the edges the no-op sentinel produces no NavigateMsg.
- `internal/store/articles_test.go` — add `TestArticles_NeighboursOf`
  covering: middle article returns both neighbours, first article
  returns prev=0, last article returns next=0, single-article board
  returns both 0, wrong boardID returns 0/0.

### 1.2 Article pagination — `g` / `G`

`PgUp`/`PgDn` already exist as `b`/`pgup` and space/`pgdown` aliases
(see `screen_article_view.go:79-88`). Only `g` (top) and `G` (bottom)
are missing.

**Files to modify**:

- `internal/tui/screen_article_view.go:79-88` — add `case "g":
  m.scroll = 0` and `case "G": m.scroll = max(0, len(bodyLines) -
  viewportHeight)`. Compute `viewportHeight` the same way the existing
  paging keys do.

**Tests**:

- `internal/tui/screen_article_view_test.go` — add a table for scroll
  keys (`["g","G","b","pgup","space","pgdown"]`) asserting the resulting
  `m.scroll` value against a fixture article with N body lines.

### 1.3 Online-user list screen

**Files to create**:

- `internal/tui/screen_online.go` — new model `onlineModel`. Init pulls
  `m.deps.Broker.OnlineList()` (returns `[]chat.OnlineUser`), renders
  one line per user: `userid (N sessions)`. Cursor `j`/`k`, `h`/esc back
  to main menu, `l`/enter optionally opens a water-balloon compose
  pre-filled with the recipient (mirrors `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose,
  Recipient: ...}`).

**Files to modify**:

---

## Phase 2 — Two `[M]` features

### 2.1 Live broadcast of new articles (`ArticleAddedMsg`)

Direct mirror of the `PushAddedMsg` pattern.

**Files to modify**:

- `internal/tui/messages.go:36-44` — add:
  ```go
  type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
      BoardID    int64
      ArticleID  int64
      AuthorUserID string
      Title      string
  }
  ```
- `internal/tui/screen_post_compose.go:101-124` (`submit`) —
  - Capture the `*Article` returned by `Articles().Create()` (currently
    discarded on line 114).
  - After `IncrementPosts` succeeds, call
    `m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleAddedMsg{BoardID: m.boardID,
    ArticleID: art.ID, AuthorUserID: u.UserID, Title: art.Title})`
    if `m.deps.Broker != nil`.
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go` — handle `ArticleAddedMsg`:
  ```go
  case ArticleAddedMsg:
      if msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
          // re-fetch article list from DB (canonical state)
          if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID); err == nil {
              m.articles = arts
          }
      }
  ```
  Mirrors the `PushAddedMsg` re-fetch pattern in `screen_article_view.go:54-66`.

**Tests**:

- `internal/tui/screen_post_compose_test.go` — assert that a successful
  submit returns a `tea.BatchMsg` (or sequence) that includes both the
  `NavigateMsg` and a broker `SendToAll` invocation. Use a fake broker
  satisfying `chat.Sender`-like semantics (or expose a recording broker
  via the existing `Sender` interface).
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view_test.go` — feed an `ArticleAddedMsg`
  with matching `BoardID` and assert `m.articles` was re-fetched (one
  more entry); feed a non-matching `BoardID` and assert no change.

### 2.2 Mailbox 站內信

Persistent threaded messages — distinct from water balloons (transient
toast). Direct structural analog of the WaterBalloon repo + screens, plus
a thread/parent column.

**Files to create**:

- `internal/store/migrations/0003_add_mail.sql`:
  ```sql
  CREATE TABLE mail (
      id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      thread_id       INTEGER,                  -- root mail id; NULL ⇒ self-root
      parent_id       INTEGER REFERENCES mail(id),
      from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
      from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,
      to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
      subject         TEXT NOT NULL,
      body            TEXT NOT NULL,
      read_at         DATETIME,
      created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
  );
  CREATE INDEX idx_mail_to_unread ON mail(to_user_id, read_at);
  CREATE INDEX idx_mail_thread ON mail(thread_id);
  ```
  On insert, if `parent_id IS NULL` then `thread_id = id` (set with a
  follow-up UPDATE inside the same transaction, like `PushRepo.Create`'s
  two-statement pattern).

- `internal/store/mail.go` — `MailRepo` mirroring `WaterBalloonRepo`
  (see `internal/store/waterballoons.go`):
  - `Insert(ctx, fromID, fromUserID, toID, subject, body, parentID *int64) (*Mail, error)`
  - `ListInboxFor(ctx, toUserID, limit int) ([]*Mail, error)` — unread first,
    ordered by `(read_at IS NULL) DESC, id DESC`
  - `ListThread(ctx, threadID int64) ([]*Mail, error)` — chronological
  - `MarkRead(ctx, id int64) error`
  - `MarkAllReadFor(ctx, toUserID int64) error`
  - All write methods grab `Store.writeMu` (process-level mutex
    discipline — see `CLAUDE.md` for why).

- `internal/tui/screen_mail_inbox.go` — list view (subject, from,
  date, status). Cursor `j`/`k`, enter/`l` opens thread view, `c`
  composes new, `r` replies to highlighted, `h`/esc back.
- `internal/tui/screen_mail_thread.go` — chronological list of one
  thread; `r` to reply (pre-fills `parent_id` and `subject` with `Re:`
  prefix if not already present); `h`/esc back to inbox.
- `internal/tui/screen_mail_compose.go` — three-field form: To,
  Subject, Body (vs WB's two-field). `Tab` cycles focus, `Ctrl+S`
  sends. Optionally pre-fill recipient/parent_id from a NavigateMsg.

**Files to modify**:

- `internal/store/store.go:25,51,65` — add `mail *MailRepo` field,
  initialize in `Open()`, add `Mail()` accessor.
- `internal/tui/messages.go` — add `ScreenMailInbox`, `ScreenMailThread`,
  `ScreenMailCompose` constants. Extend `NavigateMsg` with `MailID` and
  `MailThreadID` fields if the existing ones (`BoardID`, `ArticleID`,
  `Recipient`) don't suffice for thread-view routing.
- `internal/tui/root.go:109-141` — add `case ScreenMailInbox/Thread/Compose`
  arms.
- `internal/tui/screen_main_menu.go` — add a "信箱 Mail" item (slot 3
  if 1.3 hasn't shipped; slot 4 otherwise; bump Quit accordingly).
- `internal/server/server.go` — on session login, fetch unread mail
  count via `MailRepo.CountUnreadFor` and surface as a one-time toast
  on the main menu (mirrors the on-reconnect water balloon replay
  pattern at server.go).

**Tests**:

- `internal/store/mail_test.go` — mirrors `waterballoons_test.go`:
  Insert (root + reply), thread-id self-fix on root, ListInboxFor
  ordering, ListThread chronological, MarkRead, MarkAllReadFor.
  Use `storetest.New(t)` and `storetest.MustUser()`.
- `internal/tui/screen_mail_inbox_test.go`,
  `internal/tui/screen_mail_thread_test.go`,
  `internal/tui/screen_mail_compose_test.go` — `Update()` tests
  mirroring `screen_wb_test.go`. Cover keybinding tables, navigation,
  send-success flow.

---

## Resolved decisions

- **Scope** — implement all five P1 items in this batch (Phase 1 + Phase 2).
- **`r` on board view** — dropped. Enter/`l` already opens the article;
  `r`/`R` reply is reserved for article view in a later batch so we
  don't preempt PTT's reply-on-article-view convention.
- **`[`/`]` on article view** — *does* navigate to prev/next sibling
  article via the new `ArticleRepo.NeighboursOf` method. Tests land
  in `articles_test.go` alongside the existing repo tests.

## Still to decide (cheap; resolve during implementation)

- **Mailbox unread badge on main menu** — whether to render `信箱 Mail (N)`
  when unread > 0. Adds a `MailRepo.CountUnreadFor` call on main-menu
  Init plus a `MailAddedMsg` listener for live refresh. Cheap to add
  later; not a blocker for the first cut.

---

## Verification

After each phase:

```bash
make test-race                # CI standard — must stay green
scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only TODO.md
```

Manual smoke (two SSH sessions, `alice` + `bob`):

- **Phase 1.1**: open board list, press `[`/`]` — cursor moves; press
  `Q` — back to main menu.
- **Phase 1.2**: open a long article (use seed-data CLI or paste a
  long body via post compose), press `g`/`G` — scroll jumps to top/bottom.
- **Phase 1.3**: connect both sessions; press `3` from main menu in
  either — sees the other (and self) in the online list.
- **Phase 2.1**: alice on `screen_board_view` of board X; bob posts
  to board X — alice's list refreshes within ~1 frame without
  re-entering. bob posts to board Y while alice is on X — alice's
  list does NOT refresh.
- **Phase 2.2**: alice sends a mail to bob; bob disconnects, reconnects,
  sees unread on main menu, reads in inbox; bob replies; alice sees the
  thread on next inbox open. Concurrent insert × 50 race test on
  `MailRepo.Insert` mirrors `TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`.

After each item ships:

```bash
scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<one-liner>"
```

1	package tui

11	)

13	type boardListModel struct {
14		deps    Deps
15		boards  []*store.Board
16		cursor  int
17		width   int
18		height  int
19		loadErr error
20	}
21	
22	func newBoardListModel(deps Deps) boardListModel {
23		boards, err := deps.Store.Boards().List(context.Background())
24		return boardListModel{deps: deps, boards: boards, loadErr: err}
25	}
26	
27	func (m boardListModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
28	
29	func (m boardListModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
30		switch msg := msg.(type) {
31		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
32			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
33			return m, nil
34		case tea.KeyMsg:
35			switch msg.String() {
36			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
37				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
38			case "up", "k":
39				if m.cursor > 0 {
40					m.cursor--
41				}
42			case "down", "j":
43				if m.cursor < len(m.boards)-1 {
44					m.cursor++
45				}
46			case "home", "g":
47				m.cursor = 0
48			case "end", "G":
49				if len(m.boards) > 0 {
50					m.cursor = len(m.boards) - 1
51				}
52			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
53				if len(m.boards) == 0 {
54					return m, nil
55				}
56				b := m.boards[m.cursor]
57				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: b.ID} }
58			}
59		}
60		return m, nil
61	}
62	
63	func (m boardListModel) View() string {
64		var b strings.Builder
65		b.WriteString("\n")
66		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板列表 Boards "))
67		b.WriteString("\n\n")
68	
69		if m.loadErr != nil {
70			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ load failed: "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
71			return b.String()
72		}
73		if len(m.boards) == 0 {
74			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no boards yet)") + "\n")
75			return b.String()
76		}
77	
78		const (
79			nameW    = 14
80			titleW   = 32
81		)
82		header := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s  %s",
83			PadRight("Name", nameW),
84			PadRight("Title", titleW),
85			"Description",
86		)
87		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
88	
89		for i, br := range m.boards {
90			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s  %s",
91				PadRight(br.Name, nameW),
92				PadRight(br.Title, titleW),
93				Truncate(br.Description, max(20, m.width-nameW-titleW-8)),
94			)
95			if i == m.cursor {
96				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
97			} else {
98				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
99			}
100		}
101	
102		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"))
103		b.WriteString("\n")
104		return b.String()
105	}
106

44			case "up", "k":

129				Truncate(a.Title, titleW),
130			)
131			if i == m.cursor {
132				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
133			} else {
134				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
135			}
136		}
137	
138		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"))
139		b.WriteString("\n")
140		return b.String()
141	}
142

1	package tui

3	import (
4		"context"

8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"

13	
14	type articleViewModel struct {
15		deps    Deps
16		article *store.Article
17		pushes  []*store.Push
18		width   int
19		height  int
20		scroll  int
21		loadErr error
22	
23		// push input state
24		pushing  bool
25		pushKind store.PushKind
26		pushIn   textinput.Model
27		err      string
28	}
29	
30	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
31		ctx := context.Background()
32		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
33		if err != nil {
34			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
35		}
36		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
37	
38		ti := textinput.New()
39		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
40		ti.CharLimit = 80
41		ti.Width = 60
42	
43		return articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti}
44	}
45	
46	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
47	
48	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
49		switch msg := msg.(type) {
50		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
51			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
52			return m, nil
53	
54		case PushAddedMsg:
55			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
56				ctx := context.Background()
57				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
58				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
59				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
60					m.pushes = pushes
61				}
62				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
63					m.article = a
64				}
65			}
66			return m, nil
67	
68		case tea.KeyMsg:
69			if m.pushing {
70				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
71			}
72			switch msg.String() {
73			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
74				boardID := int64(0)
75				if m.article != nil {
76					boardID = m.article.BoardID
77				}
78				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
79			case "up", "k":
80				if m.scroll > 0 {
81					m.scroll--
82				}
83			case "down", "j":
84				m.scroll++
85			case "pgup", "b":
86				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
87			case "pgdown", " ":
88				m.scroll += 10
89			case "+":
90				return m.openPush(store.PushKindPush), nil
91			case "-":
92				return m.openPush(store.PushKindBoo), nil
93			case "=":
94				return m.openPush(store.PushKindArrow), nil
95			}
96		}
97		return m, nil
98	}
99	
100	func (m articleViewModel) openPush(k store.PushKind) tea.Model {
101		m.pushing = true
102		m.pushKind = k
103		m.pushIn.SetValue("")
104		m.pushIn.Focus()
105		m.err = ""
106		return m
107	}
108	
109	func (m articleViewModel) updatePushInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
110		switch msg.String() {
111		case "esc":
112			m.pushing = false
113			m.pushIn.Blur()
114			return m, nil
115		case "enter":
116			body := strings.TrimSpace(m.pushIn.Value())
117			if body == "" && m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
118				m.err = "comment required for 推/噓"
119				return m, nil
120			}
121			ctx := context.Background()
122			u := m.deps.User
123			p, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.UserID, m.pushKind, body)
124			if err != nil {
125				m.err = err.Error()
126				return m, nil
127			}
128			// Append locally so the originator sees it immediately.
129			m.pushes = append(m.pushes, p)
130			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
131				m.article = a
132			}
133			m.pushing = false
134			m.pushIn.Blur()
135			// Broadcast to other live sessions; they filter by ArticleID.
136			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
137				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
138					ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
139					UserUserID: u.UserID,
140					Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
141					Body:       body,
142				})
143			}
144			return m, nil
145		}
146		var cmd tea.Cmd
147		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
148		return m, cmd
149	}
150	
151	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
152		if m.loadErr != nil {
153			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
154		}
155		if m.article == nil {
156			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
157		}
158		a := m.article
159	
160		var b strings.Builder
161		b.WriteString("\n")
162		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
163		b.WriteString("\n\n")
164	
165		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
166		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
167		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
168		b.WriteString("\n")
169	
170		bodyLines := strings.Split(a.Body, "\n")
171		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
172		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
173		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
174		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
175			b.WriteString("  " + line + "\n")
176		}
177		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
178			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
179			b.WriteString("\n")
180		}
181	
182		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
183			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
184			for _, p := range m.pushes {
185				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
186				line := fmt.Sprintf("  %s %s %s  %s",
187					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
188					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
189					p.Body,
190					StyleDim.Render(ts),
191				)
192				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
193			}
194		}
195	
196		if m.pushing {
197			b.WriteString("\n  ")
198			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
199			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
200			b.WriteString("\n")
201			if m.err != "" {
202				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
203			}
204			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
205		} else {
206			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("j/k scroll · + 推 · - 噓 · = → · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"))
207		}
208		b.WriteString("\n")
209		return b.String()
210	}
211	
212	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
213		switch k {
214		case store.PushKindPush:
215			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
216		case store.PushKindBoo:
217			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
218		case store.PushKindArrow:
219			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
220		}
221		return "?"
222	}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/articles.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Article struct {
11		ID              int64
12		BoardID         int64
13		AuthorID        int64
14		AuthorUserID    string
15		Title           string
16		Body            string
17		RecommendScore  int64
18		Filemode        int64
19		CreatedAt       time.Time
20	}
21	
22	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
23	
24	var ErrArticleNotFound = errors.New("article not found")
25	
26	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
27		recommend_score, filemode, created_at`
28	
29	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
30		a := &Article{}
31		err := row.Scan(
32			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
33			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt,
34		)
35		return a, err
36	}
37	
38	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
39		if limit <= 0 {
40			limit = 50
41		}
42		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
43			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
44			boardID, limit,
45		)
46		if err != nil {
47			return nil, err
48		}
49		defer rows.Close()
50		var out []*Article
51		for rows.Next() {
52			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
53			if err != nil {
54				return nil, err
55			}
56			out = append(out, a)
57		}
58		return out, rows.Err()
59	}
60	
61	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
62		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
63		a, err := scanArticle(row)
64		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
65			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
66		}
67		return a, err
68	}
69	
70	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
71		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
72		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
73		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
74			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
75			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
76		)
77		if err != nil {
78			return nil, err
79		}
80		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
81		if err != nil {
82			return nil, err
83		}
84		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
85		return scanArticle(row)
86	}
87
```

1	package tui
2	
3	// Screen identifies which sub-model the Root should be showing.
4	type Screen int
5	
6	const (
7		ScreenMainMenu Screen = iota
8		ScreenBoardList
9		ScreenBoardView
10		ScreenArticleView
11		ScreenPostCompose
12		ScreenWBInbox
13		ScreenWBCompose
14	)
15	
16	// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.
17	// Optional fields carry context the destination screen needs.
18	type NavigateMsg struct {
19		To        Screen
20		BoardID   int64
21		ArticleID int64
22		Recipient string // for ScreenWBCompose: prefilled recipient userid
23	}
24	
25	// ErrorMsg surfaces an error as a transient toast.
26	type ErrorMsg struct{ Err error }
27	
28	// WBIncomingMsg is delivered by the chat broker (or replayed from DB on Init)
29	// to notify the recipient that a new water balloon arrived.
30	type WBIncomingMsg struct {
31		ID         int64
32		FromUserID string
33		Body       string
34	}
35	
36	// PushAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a push is added to an
37	// article. The article-view model checks ArticleID to decide whether to
38	// re-render.
39	type PushAddedMsg struct {
40		ArticleID  int64
41		UserUserID string
42		Kind       string
43		Body       string
44	}
45

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/root.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"time"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8	
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
14	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
15	type Deps struct {
16		Store      *store.Store
17		Broker     *chat.Broker // nil during register
18		User       *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister
19		IsRegister bool
20	}
21	
22	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
23	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
24	// and the toast footer.
25	type Root struct {
26		deps   Deps
27		sub    tea.Model
28		width  int
29		height int
30	
31		toast      string
32		toastUntil time.Time
33	}
34	
35	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
36	
37	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
38	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
39		r := Root{deps: deps}
40		if deps.IsRegister {
41			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
42		} else {
43			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
44		}
45		return r
46	}
47	
48	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
49		return m.sub.Init()
50	}
51	
52	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
53		switch msg := msg.(type) {
54		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
55			m.width = msg.Width
56			m.height = msg.Height
57			var cmd tea.Cmd
58			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
59			return m, cmd
60	
61		case tea.KeyMsg:
62			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
63			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
64				return m, tea.Quit
65			}
66			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
67			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
68				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
69			}
70	
71		case NavigateMsg:
72			return m.navigate(msg)
73	
74		case ErrorMsg:
75			if msg.Err != nil {
76				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
77			}
78			return m, nil
79	
80		case WBIncomingMsg:
81			m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
82			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect.
83			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
84				go func(id int64) {
85					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
86				}(msg.ID)
87			}
88			// fall through so the active screen can also react
89		}
90	
91		var cmd tea.Cmd
92		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
93		return m, cmd
94	}
95	
96	func (m Root) View() string {
97		v := m.sub.View()
98		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
99			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
100		}
101		return v
102	}
103	
104	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
105		m.toast = s
106		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
107	}
108	
109	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
110		var sub tea.Model
111		switch n.To {
112		case ScreenMainMenu:
113			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
114		case ScreenBoardList:
115			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
116		case ScreenBoardView:
117			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
118		case ScreenArticleView:
119			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
120		case ScreenPostCompose:
121			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
122		case ScreenWBInbox:
123			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
124		case ScreenWBCompose:
125			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
126		default:
127			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
128			return m, nil
129		}
130		m.sub = sub
131		cmd := sub.Init()
132		if m.width > 0 {
133			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
134			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
135			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
136			if cmd2 != nil {
137				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
138			}
139		}
140		return m, cmd
141	}
142

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8	)
9	
10	type menuItem struct {
11		label string
12		hint  string
13		to    Screen
14	}
15	
16	type mainMenuModel struct {
17		deps   Deps
18		items  []menuItem
19		cursor int
20	}
21	
22	func newMainMenuModel(deps Deps) mainMenuModel {
23		return mainMenuModel{
24			deps: deps,
25			items: []menuItem{
26				{label: "看板列表 Boards", hint: "browse and read articles", to: ScreenBoardList},
27				{label: "水球 Water Balloons", hint: "private messages with online users", to: ScreenWBInbox},
28				{label: "離線 Quit", hint: "disconnect", to: -1},
29			},
30		}
31	}
32	
33	func (m mainMenuModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m mainMenuModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
37			switch k.String() {
38			case "q", "esc":
39				return m, tea.Quit
40			case "up", "k":
41				if m.cursor > 0 {
42					m.cursor--
43				}
44				return m, nil
45			case "down", "j":
46				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
47					m.cursor++
48				}
49				return m, nil
50			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
51				it := m.items[m.cursor]
52				if it.to == -1 {
53					return m, tea.Quit
54				}
55				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
56			case "1", "2", "3":
57				idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
58				if idx < len(m.items) {
59					it := m.items[idx]
60					if it.to == -1 {
61						return m, tea.Quit
62					}
63					return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
64				}
65			}
66		}
67		return m, nil
68	}
69	
70	func (m mainMenuModel) View() string {
71		u := m.deps.User
72		nick := u.Nickname
73		if nick == "" {
74			nick = u.UserID
75		}
76	
77		var b strings.Builder
78		b.WriteString("\n")
79		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" SSH-BBS · %s (%s) ", nick, u.UserID)))
80		b.WriteString("\n\n")
81	
82		if u.LastLoginAt.Valid {
83			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("上次登入 %s · 累計登入 %d 次 · 發文 %d 篇",
84				u.LastLoginAt.Time.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
85				u.NumLogins,
86				u.NumPosts,
87			)))
88			b.WriteString("\n\n")
89		}
90	
91		b.WriteString("  ")
92		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 主選單 Main Menu "))
93		b.WriteString("\n\n")
94	
95		for i, it := range m.items {
96			marker := "  "
97			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
98			if i == m.cursor {
99				marker = "▸ "
100				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-32s ", i+1, it.label))
101			}
102			b.WriteString(marker + row)
103			if it.hint != "" {
104				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
105			}
106			b.WriteString("\n")
107		}
108	
109		b.WriteString("\n  ")
110		b.WriteString(StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-3 jump · q quit"))
111		b.WriteString("\n")
112		return b.String()
113	}
114

12	func seedArticle(t *testing.T) (Deps, *store.Article) {
13		t.Helper()
14		st := storetest.New(t)
15		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "Alice")
16		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
17		a, err := st.Articles().Create(context.Background(), board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "title", "line1\nline2\nline3")
18		if err != nil {
19			t.Fatalf("Create article: %v", err)
20		}
21		return Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: chat.NewBroker()}, a
22	}
23	
24	func TestArticleView_LoadsArticle(t *testing.T) {
25		deps, art := seedArticle(t)
26		m := newArticleViewModel(deps, art.ID)
27		if m.loadErr != nil {
28			t.Fatalf("loadErr = %v", m.loadErr)
29		}
30		if m.article.ID != art.ID {
31			t.Errorf("article.ID = %d, want %d", m.article.ID, art.ID)
32		}
33	}
34	
35	func TestArticleView_BackKeys(t *testing.T) {
36		deps, art := seedArticle(t)
37		for _, key := range []string{"esc", "backspace", "left", "h"} {
38			t.Run(key, func(t *testing.T) {
39				m := newArticleViewModel(deps, art.ID)
40				_, cmd := m.Update(keyOf(key))
41				msg := runCmd(cmd)
42				nav, ok := msg.(NavigateMsg)
43				if !ok {
44					t.Fatalf("got %T, want NavigateMsg", msg)
45				}
46				if nav.To != ScreenBoardView {
47					t.Errorf("To = %v, want ScreenBoardView", nav.To)
48				}
49				if nav.BoardID != art.BoardID {
50					t.Errorf("BoardID = %d, want %d", nav.BoardID, art.BoardID)
51				}
52			})
53		}
54	}
55	
56	// '+' / '-' / '=' open the inline push input with the right kind.
57	func TestArticleView_OpenPushKinds(t *testing.T) {
58		cases := []struct {
59			key  string
60			kind store.PushKind
61		}{
62			{"+", store.PushKindPush},
63			{"-", store.PushKindBoo},
64			{"=", store.PushKindArrow},
65		}
66		for _, tc := range cases {
67			t.Run(string(tc.kind), func(t *testing.T) {
68				deps, art := seedArticle(t)
69				m := newArticleViewModel(deps, art.ID)
70				model, _ := m.Update(keyOf(tc.key))
71				got := model.(articleViewModel)
72				if !got.pushing {
73					t.Errorf("pushing=false after %q", tc.key)
74				}
75				if got.pushKind != tc.kind {
76					t.Errorf("pushKind = %s, want %s", got.pushKind, tc.kind)
77				}
78			})
79		}
80	}
81	
82	// PushAddedMsg for a different article must NOT mutate this view —
83	// guards against the broadcast-to-all + filter-by-id pattern from Step 7.
84	func TestArticleView_IgnoresUnrelatedPush(t *testing.T) {
85		deps, art := seedArticle(t)
86		m := newArticleViewModel(deps, art.ID)
87	
88		model, _ := m.Update(PushAddedMsg{
89			ArticleID:  art.ID + 999, // some other article
90			UserUserID: "bob",
91			Kind:       string(store.PushKindPush),
92			Body:       "irrelevant",
93		})
94		got := model.(articleViewModel)
95		if len(got.pushes) != 0 {
96			t.Errorf("appended unrelated push: got %d, want 0", len(got.pushes))
97		}
98	}
99	
100	// PushAddedMsg for THIS article triggers a re-fetch so timestamps and
101	// recommend_score reflect canonical DB state.
102	func TestArticleView_AcceptsRelatedPush(t *testing.T) {
103		deps, art := seedArticle(t)
104		// Insert a push directly so the re-fetch sees something.
105		if _, err := deps.Store.Pushes().Create(context.Background(), art.ID, deps.User.ID, deps.User.UserID, store.PushKindPush, "from outside"); err != nil {
106			t.Fatalf("seed push: %v", err)
107		}
108		m := newArticleViewModel(deps, art.ID)
109		// At this point m.pushes has 1 from the constructor's load.
110		if len(m.pushes) != 1 {
111			t.Fatalf("constructor loaded %d pushes, want 1", len(m.pushes))
112		}
113	
114		// Insert a SECOND push externally, then fire PushAddedMsg.
115		if _, err := deps.Store.Pushes().Create(context.Background(), art.ID, deps.User.ID, deps.User.UserID, store.PushKindBoo, "another"); err != nil {
116			t.Fatalf("seed push 2: %v", err)
117		}
118		model, _ := m.Update(PushAddedMsg{ArticleID: art.ID, UserUserID: "bob", Kind: "boo", Body: "another"})
119		got := model.(articleViewModel)
120	
121		if len(got.pushes) != 2 {
122			t.Errorf("after PushAddedMsg: %d pushes, want 2", len(got.pushes))
123		}
124		if got.article.RecommendScore != 0 { // +1 push then -1 boo
125			t.Errorf("RecommendScore = %d, want 0", got.article.RecommendScore)
126		}
127	}

98	func contains(s, sub string) bool {
99		return len(s) >= len(sub) && (s == sub || indexOf(s, sub) >= 0)
100	}
101	
102	func indexOf(s, sub string) int {
103		for i := 0; i+len(sub) <= len(s); i++ {
104			if s[i:i+len(sub)] == sub {
105				return i
106			}
107		}
108		return -1
109	}

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"testing"
5	
6		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
9	)
10	
11	// keyOf builds a tea.KeyMsg whose .String() matches the wanted form.
12	// bubbletea exposes typed key constants; we use Runes for ASCII letters
13	// and named Type values for special keys.
14	func keyOf(s string) tea.KeyMsg {
15		switch s {
16		case "enter":
17			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEnter}
18		case "esc":
19			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEsc}
20		case "left":
21			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyLeft}
22		case "right":
23			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRight}
24		case "up":
25			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyUp}
26		case "down":
27			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown}
28		case "backspace":
29			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyBackspace}
30		case "ctrl+u":
31			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyCtrlU}
32		case "ctrl+c":
33			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyCtrlC}
34		case " ":
35			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeySpace}
36		}
37		return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune(s)}
38	}
39	
40	// runCmd invokes the tea.Cmd returned by Update and returns the resulting
41	// tea.Msg (or nil if cmd is nil).
42	func runCmd(cmd tea.Cmd) tea.Msg {
43		if cmd == nil {
44			return nil
45		}
46		return cmd()
47	}
48	
49	func newMainMenuFixture() (mainMenuModel, Deps) {
50		deps := Deps{
51			User: &store.User{ID: 1, UserID: "alice", Nickname: "Alice"},
52		}
53		return newMainMenuModel(deps), deps
54	}
55	
56	func TestMainMenu_CursorMovement(t *testing.T) {
57		m, _ := newMainMenuFixture()
58		if m.cursor != 0 {
59			t.Fatalf("initial cursor = %d, want 0", m.cursor)
60		}
61		for _, key := range []string{"j", "down"} {
62			m, _ = updateMM(m, keyOf(key))
63		}
64		if m.cursor != 2 {
65			t.Errorf("after 2x down: cursor = %d, want 2", m.cursor)
66		}
67		m, _ = updateMM(m, keyOf("k"))
68		m, _ = updateMM(m, keyOf("up"))
69		if m.cursor != 0 {
70			t.Errorf("after 2x up: cursor = %d, want 0", m.cursor)
71		}
72	}
73	
74	func TestMainMenu_CursorClamps(t *testing.T) {
75		m, _ := newMainMenuFixture()
76		for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
77			m, _ = updateMM(m, keyOf("k")) // should not go below 0
78		}
79		if m.cursor != 0 {
80			t.Errorf("cursor underflowed: %d", m.cursor)
81		}
82		for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
83			m, _ = updateMM(m, keyOf("j")) // should not go past last
84		}
85		if m.cursor != len(m.items)-1 {
86			t.Errorf("cursor overflowed: %d, want %d", m.cursor, len(m.items)-1)
87		}
88	}
89	
90	// "l" / right / Enter all open the cursored item — verifies the new
91	// h/l navigation parity for forward-direction.
92	func TestMainMenu_ForwardKeys(t *testing.T) {
93		for _, key := range []string{"enter", " ", "l", "right"} {
94			t.Run(key, func(t *testing.T) {
95				m, _ := newMainMenuFixture()
96				// cursor=0 -> first item routes to ScreenBoardList
97				_, cmd := updateMM(m, keyOf(key))
98				msg := runCmd(cmd)
99				nav, ok := msg.(NavigateMsg)
100				if !ok {
101					t.Fatalf("got %T, want NavigateMsg", msg)
102				}
103				if nav.To != ScreenBoardList {
104					t.Errorf("To = %v, want ScreenBoardList", nav.To)
105				}
106			})
107		}
108	}
109	
110	func TestMainMenu_NumericShortcuts(t *testing.T) {
111		cases := []struct {
112			key  string
113			want Screen
114		}{
115			{"1", ScreenBoardList},
116			{"2", ScreenWBInbox},
117		}
118		for _, tc := range cases {
119			t.Run(tc.key, func(t *testing.T) {
120				m, _ := newMainMenuFixture()
121				_, cmd := updateMM(m, keyOf(tc.key))
122				msg := runCmd(cmd)
123				nav, ok := msg.(NavigateMsg)
124				if !ok {
125					t.Fatalf("got %T, want NavigateMsg", msg)
126				}
127				if nav.To != tc.want {
128					t.Errorf("To = %v, want %v", nav.To, tc.want)
129				}
130			})
131		}
132	}
133	
134	// Quit shortcut: '3' jumps to "Quit" and emits tea.Quit, not a NavigateMsg.
135	func TestMainMenu_QuitShortcut(t *testing.T) {
136		m, _ := newMainMenuFixture()
137		_, cmd := updateMM(m, keyOf("3"))
138		msg := runCmd(cmd)
139		if _, isQuit := msg.(tea.QuitMsg); !isQuit {
140			t.Errorf("got %T, want tea.QuitMsg", msg)
141		}
142	}
143	
144	// Helper to type-assert past the tea.Model interface back to mainMenuModel.
145	func updateMM(m mainMenuModel, msg tea.Msg) (mainMenuModel, tea.Cmd) {
146		model, cmd := m.Update(msg)
147		return model.(mainMenuModel), cmd
148	}

1	// Package storetest provides shared helpers for tests that need a fresh
2	// SQLite store. It lives in its own package so any internal package can
3	// import it without creating cycles.
4	package storetest
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"testing"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// New opens a fresh on-disk SQLite store in t.TempDir(), runs migrations,
15	// and registers a Cleanup to close it. Use it whenever you'd write
16	// `store.Open` in a test — never share stores across tests.
17	func New(tb testing.TB) *store.Store {
18		tb.Helper()
19		dir := tb.TempDir()
20		st, err := store.Open(filepath.Join(dir, "test.db"))
21		if err != nil {
22			tb.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
23		}
24		tb.Cleanup(func() { _ = st.Close() })
25		return st
26	}
27	
28	// MustUser inserts a user with a placeholder password hash and returns it.
29	// Convenient for tests that need a logged-in identity but don't care about auth.
30	func MustUser(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, userID, nickname string) *store.User {
31		tb.Helper()
32		u, err := st.Users().Create(context.Background(), userID, "$2a$12$placeholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholder.", nickname, "")
33		if err != nil {
34			tb.Fatalf("create user %q: %v", userID, err)
35		}
36		return u
37	}
38	
39	// MustBoard returns a seeded board by name, failing the test if not found.
40	// Useful with the default Welcome / Test / ChitChat boards which
41	// SeedDefaults / migration 0002 create automatically.
42	func MustBoard(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, name string) *store.Board {
43		tb.Helper()
44		b, err := st.Boards().GetByName(context.Background(), name)
45		if err != nil {
46			tb.Fatalf("get board %q: %v", name, err)
47		}
48		return b
49	}
50

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Board struct {
11		ID          int64
12		Name        string
13		Title       string
14		Description string
15		BM          string
16		Attr        int64
17		CreatedAt   time.Time
18	}
19	
20	type BoardRepo struct{ s *Store }
21	
22	var ErrBoardNotFound = errors.New("board not found")
23	
24	const boardColumns = `id, name, title, description, bm, attr, created_at`
25	
26	func scanBoard(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Board, error) {
27		b := &Board{}
28		err := row.Scan(&b.ID, &b.Name, &b.Title, &b.Description, &b.BM, &b.Attr, &b.CreatedAt)
29		return b, err
30	}
31	
32	func (r *BoardRepo) List(ctx context.Context) ([]*Board, error) {
33		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
34			`SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards ORDER BY name COLLATE NOCASE`)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, err
37		}
38		defer rows.Close()
39		var out []*Board
40		for rows.Next() {
41			b, err := scanBoard(rows)
42			if err != nil {
43				return nil, err
44			}
45			out = append(out, b)
46		}
47		return out, rows.Err()
48	}
49	
50	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Board, error) {
51		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, id)
52		b, err := scanBoard(row)
53		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
54			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
55		}
56		return b, err
57	}
58	
59	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (*Board, error) {
60		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE name = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, name)
61		b, err := scanBoard(row)
62		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
63			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
64		}
65		return b, err
66	}
67	
68	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs
69	// every startup; INSERT OR IGNORE makes it a no-op once the rows exist.
70	func (r *BoardRepo) SeedDefaults(ctx context.Context) error {
71		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
72		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
73		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
74			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO boards (name, title, description) VALUES
75				('Welcome',  '歡迎來到 SSH-BBS', 'System welcome and announcements'),
76				('Test',     '測試板',           'Try things here. Posts may be cleared periodically.'),
77				('ChitChat', '閒聊',             'General chat. Be kind.')
78		`)
79		return err
80	}
81

package tui

import (
	"context"
	"testing"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
)

func newBoardViewFixture(t *testing.T) (boardViewModel, *store.Board, []*store.Article) {
	t.Helper()
	st := storetest.New(t)
	user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
	board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
	var arts []*store.Article
	for _, title := range []string{"first", "second", "third"} {
		a, err := st.Articles().Create(context.Background(), board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, title, "")
		if err != nil {
			t.Fatalf("create %s: %v", title, err)
		}
		arts = append(arts, a)
	}
	deps := Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: chat.NewBroker()}
	return newBoardViewModel(deps, board.ID), board, arts
}

// `[` and `]` move the cursor up/down the article list; matches j/k aliases.
func TestBoardView_BracketCursorAliases(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newBoardViewFixture(t)
	if m.cursor != 0 {
		t.Fatalf("initial cursor = %d, want 0", m.cursor)
	}
	model, _ := m.Update(keyOf("]"))
	m = model.(boardViewModel)
	if m.cursor != 1 {
		t.Errorf("after ]: cursor = %d, want 1", m.cursor)
	}
	model, _ = m.Update(keyOf("["))
	m = model.(boardViewModel)
	if m.cursor != 0 {
		t.Errorf("after [: cursor = %d, want 0", m.cursor)
	}
}

// `Q` from board view jumps straight to main menu (skipping board list).
func TestBoardView_QuitToMenu(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newBoardViewFixture(t)
	_, cmd := m.Update(keyOf("Q"))
	msg := runCmd(cmd)
	nav, ok := msg.(NavigateMsg)
	if !ok {
		t.Fatalf("got %T, want NavigateMsg", msg)
	}
	if nav.To != ScreenMainMenu {
		t.Errorf("To = %v, want ScreenMainMenu", nav.To)
	}
}

// All four "back" keys (esc, backspace, left, h) navigate to board list.
func TestBoardView_BackKeys(t *testing.T) {
	for _, key := range []string{"esc", "backspace", "left", "h"} {
		t.Run(key, func(t *testing.T) {
			m, _, _ := newBoardViewFixture(t)
			_, cmd := m.Update(keyOf(key))
			nav := runCmd(cmd).(NavigateMsg)
			if nav.To != ScreenBoardList {
				t.Errorf("To = %v, want ScreenBoardList", nav.To)
			}
		})
	}
}
